Patch Set 15: Verified-1 Code-Review-1

Between patch #12 and patch #13 this seems to have started hitting a permanent failure in sanity-lfsck:

 LustreError: 27736:0:(fld_handler.c:260:fld_server_lookup()) srv-lustre-MDT0000: Cannot find sequence 0x200000001: rc = -2
 BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at 0000000000000108
 [<ffffffffa04017c2>] ldiskfs_readdir+0x32/0x760 [ldiskfs]
 Pid: 27736, comm: lfsck_namespace Not tainted 2.6.32-431.29.2.el6_lustre.g846fc79.x86_64 #1 Red Hat KVM
 RIP: 0010:[<ffffffffa04017c2>]  [<ffffffffa04017c2>] ldiskfs_readdir+0x32/0x760 [ldiskfs
 Process lfsck_namespace (pid: 27736, threadinfo ffff88006d2dc000, task ffff88006623d500)
 Call Trace:
 [<ffffffffa0d09eb4>] osd_ldiskfs_it_fill+0xb4/0x230 [osd_ldiskfs]
 [<ffffffffa0d0a1c6>] osd_it_ea_next+0x96/0x190 [osd_ldiskfs]
 [<ffffffffa0e4553d>] lfsck_namespace_scan_local_lpf+0x3bd/0x16d0 [lfsck]
 [<ffffffffa0e4effb>] lfsck_namespace_assistant_handler_p2+0x6fb/0x11b0 [lfsck]
 [<ffffffffa0e31399>] lfsck_assistant_engine+0x14e9/0x1e00 [lfsck]
 [<ffffffff8109abf6>] kthread+0x96/0xa0

Is it possible that LFSCK is now using some of the fields that were thought to be separate?

Patch Set 15: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 15:

Fan Yong, would it be possible to allocate oti_idx_it for LFSCK purpose?

Patch Set 15:

> Fan Yong, would it be possible to allocate oti_idx_it for LFSCK purpose?

The LFSCK trace file uses IAM as the index file, which will uses oti_idx_it for lookup. During the LFSCK namespace traverse, it may operate on the trace file. So the oti_it_dentry and oti_idx_it may be used by the same thread at the same time, so they cannot share the same union.

On the other hand, IAM lookup will be triggered by any OI lookup, that is a critical path. It is better to pre-allocate it on the thread_info.

Patch Set 15:

ok, let me try to take it out .. btw, do I understand right that osd_it_ea_init() may share for inlined and non-inlined iterator?
Uploaded patch set 16.

Patch Set 16:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 16: Code-Review+1

> ok, let me try to take it out .. btw, do I understand right that osd_it_ea_init() may share for inlined and non-inlined iterator?

I am not sure I understand you about the "inline iterator" and "non-inline iterator". Anyway, the oti_it_dentry will be recorded in the iteration structure when osd_it_ea_init() called, and will be kept using until osd_it_ea_fini(). So during this thread iteration, if any others use the same area, or this thread itself re-uses the same area for other purpose, then it may cause this iteration failure.

parents.subject: LU-5264 obdclass: fix race during key quiescency

commit.subject: LU-3680 osd: reduce osd_thread_info in ldiskfs osd
commit.message:
LU-3680 osd: reduce osd_thread_info in ldiskfs osd

by unioning few rarely used fields. now the structure should
fit a page:

(gdb) p sizeof(struct osd_thread_info)
$1 = 3296
